title: lichen-core re-exports lichen_ipv6::Addr as Ipv6Addr but underlying type is still named Addr
description:
lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs exports:
  pub struct Addr(pub [u8; 16]);

lichen-core/src/addr.rs re-exports as:
  pub use lichen_ipv6::Addr as Ipv6Addr;

Users of lichen-core see Ipv6Addr, but:
- Error messages may show 'Addr' not 'Ipv6Addr'
- Rustdoc links to lichen_ipv6::Addr  
- IDE autocomplete may show both names
- lichen-ipv6 docs call it 'Addr' while lichen-core docs call it 'Ipv6Addr'

What goes wrong: Documentation and error messages are inconsistent. The dual naming creates confusion about whether Addr and Ipv6Addr are the same type. Consider renaming the type to Ipv6Addr at the source.
close_reason: Cosmetic naming issue - renaming Addr to Ipv6Addr at source would be a breaking change. Re-export as Ipv6Addr is correct practice.
